question: I'm new to Yoga and/or Pilates, which class is the best to start with?
answer:
Hi Friend! Welcome! Many of our students are beginners to yoga and mat pilates. Take a look at our current schedule. For Yoga, look for Soul Sunday or Yoga Flow. For Mat Pilates, look for Yogilates. These classes are great places to start. All classes offer options for poses/exercises so you can explore it as gently or intensely as you desire. Our classes allow for a variety of variations of postures for all levels of practitioners.
We always say try yoga at the very least three times. Check out our schedule here.

question: What if I'm not flexible?
answer:
That’s ok! We all need to start somewhere. Saying you’re not flexible enough to do yoga is like saying you’re not dirty enough to take a shower!
One reason to practice yoga asanas is to invite more flexibility.

question: What should I wear to practice?
answer: Comfortable clothing that you can move in easily is appropriate. Keep in mind shirts that are super baggy may get in the way.

question: Do I need to bring anything?
answer: Just you and maybe a water. We have all the props you will need and mats you can borrow.

question: Where are you located?
answer:
360 Main St, Suite 5, Matawan, NJ 07747
Click here for directions

Enter parking lot then drive past the first entrance you see. Turn right to park along the back. Entrance is on the right side of the building.
Once enter building, turn right & follow the purple path up the ramp.
Last door on the left.

question: Do I have to register in advance or can I drop in?
answer:
We ask that you please pre-register for classes in advance. Our classes can sometimes fill up/sell out. Registering in advance allows us to better prepare for classes. Drop-in is welcome but a spot is not guaranteed.
Register for classes here

question: What passes do you offer and how much do they cost?
answer: You can view pricing information here. For more information on passes or to purchase, click here.

question: How do I redeem my gift certificate for a single class or pack?
answer:
EMAILED Gift Certificates:

STEP 1: USE EMAILED LINK
The quickest way to purchase your pass using your gift certficate is to use the unique link included in the purchase email. Clicking that link will direct you to the correct pass and pre-fill the gift card code for checkout. If you don’t have the email, contact the person who gifted you the card to resend it.

The Gift Card email will have the subject: Your Gift Card Purchase.

STEP 2: FILL OUT PURCHASE FORM
As the gift recipient, you’ll fill out the purchase form with the required details. This allows Punchpass (our booking system) to apply the gift card to your customer account so you can book your class.

  • When following the unique link from the email, we’ll pre-fill the discount code for you.
    • If you don’t see the gift card code applied, manually enter it and press the Apply Discount button.

PHYSICAL Gift Certificates (no email link)

STEP 1: LOCATE GIFT CARD CODE
Locate the gift card code on the physical gift card. STEP 2: VISIT THE PASS PAGE TO PURCHASE
Click here to visit the pass page on ILP.punchpass.com and find the correpsonding pass to purchase & click on a pass to open the checkout page. STEP 3: FILL OUT PURCHASE FORM
As the gift recipient, you’ll fill out the form with your details: your name, your email address, and your phone number. This allows Punchpass to apply the gift card to your customer account so you can book your class. STEP 4: ENTER GIFT CARD CODE IN DISCOUNT FIELD
Your gift card code is the discount code. You’ll enter the gift card code into the discount code field and press Apply Discount.

​When a gift card code is applied, the price of the pass will be come $0.

​If the price isn’t $0, just enter the gift card code into the discount code field and click Apply Discount as described above. That will change the price of the pass to be free.

question: Can you charge my card?
answer: We do not store credit card information that we have access to use. We are unable to charge classes, passes, workshops, events, merch or beverages onto your account on your behalf.

question: What do I need for online yoga or pilates?
answer:
• A good internet connection.
  • A computer/phone/tablet with camera, microphone and speaker. Wireless headphones are recommended for the best sound experience.
  • Zoom app downloaded and installed on your computer/phone/tablet/device. Please enable “Original Sound On” in your Zoom settings. If you’re not sure how to or if this feature is enabled in your Zoom settings, please see Zoom Original Sound On Instructions. Test out your Zoom audio and video settings prior to class. The Zoom meeting ID and password or zoom link will be emailed to you at least 30 minutes before class start time.
  • A space where you will be free of distractions were you can practice yoga/pilates safely without knocking into anyone or anything.
  • An exercise (yoga/pilates) mat, cushion and blanket. Props are welcomed such as: yoga blocks (or books/toilet paper rolls/etc.) and straps (or jump rope/tie/belt/towel/etc.). For Pilates or Yogilates classes, various props may be used. You will receive an email/text prior to class with a list of props needed and alternative options. Pilates Props may include: foam roller, ankle/wrist weights, pilates ring, small 9 inch ball, large 55cm ball, loop band, straight band, stepper, roller board, 5 foot stick, slider disk, dumbbells.
  • Water to drink.

question: What can be treated with Reiki?
answer:
Reiki has had a positive affect on all forms of illness and negative conditions. This includes minor things like head or stomach aches, bee stings, colds, flu, tension and anxiety as well as serious illness like heart disease, cancer, leukemia, etc.

The side effects of regular medical treatments have also been reduced or eliminated. This includes the negative effects of chemotherapy, post operative pain and depression as well as improving the healing rate and reducing the time needed to stay in the hospital.

Reiki always helps and in some cases people have experienced complete healings which have been confirmed by medical tests before and after the Reiki treatments. Reiki is a catalyst for renewal and well-being, which can also result in healthier choices, and initiate the release of stress and negative emotions that we carry within the body.

question: Can Reiki cure me?
answer:
Reiki is not medicine—and it doesn’t treat specific symptoms or disease.

It is a stream of complimentary, intelligent energy. As such, it heads right where you need it most. Reiki works in conjunction with regular medical or psychological treatment. Reiki energy works in harmony with all other forms of healing, including drugs, surgery, psychological care or any other method of alternative care and will improve the results.

question: What results can I expect?
answer: Reiki works best when received on a regular basis. It is not the one hit wonder of healing modalities, the healing goes deeper and deeper as you receive it more regularly

question: I've booked a session. What do I wear?
answer:
Wear comfortable, non restrictive clothing.

Removing all metal jewelry and crystals so the energy can flow unadulterated is best.
In Person: Please refrain from wearing scented products: including perfume, essential oils, strong deodorants, or body sprays.

question: How do I prepare for a Reiki session?
answer:
• Prior to your session, complete and submit the Client Form.
  • Be well hydrated and not hungry. Avoid eating a heavy meal right before, but we want to make sure hunger doesn’t distract you during the session.
  • If possible, refrain from or lower your caffeine consumption the day of your treatment. Do not go into caffeine withdrawal, but instead opting for black or green tea, or dark chocolate on the day of your session.
  • Please refrain from alcohol and non prescription drugs on the day of your session. Both should also be avoided for 24-48 hours after treatment.
  • Take some time to reflect before your session. Is there an intention you would like to set for your session? Are there things you wish to discuss with me before? Are there specific areas of your body with pain, illness, or tension that you’d like me to focus on?

question: I've booked a DISTANT Reiki session. How else do I prepare?
answer:
To receive distant Reiki healing you don’t have to do anything special, but here are some recommendations to get the most out of your treatment once you’ve booked a distance Reiki session and let me know what you’d like me to work with for you,



  1. Set up your relaxation space – choose a place where you feel comfortable, relaxed, and safe. You’ll want a pillow for your head and perhaps a pillow or bolster for under your knees if your low back gets tense from lying down. If you choose to be seated, make sure you choose a comfortable armchair or recliner where you can fully relax. Grab a blanket even if your space is warm, since your body may go through temperature changes during the session and you might get chilly. An eye pillow if optional, but a great way to relax your eyes and forehead and having your eyes closed can help you focus more on your body’s sensations and feelings.
  2. Turn off notifications and limit distractions – this is your relaxation / Zen time so turn off or mute your devices, and let your family or roommates know that you’re not available during your session time. If the dog needs a walk, do that before your session so they can relax too. If you’ve chosen to use Zoom for your Distant Session, be sure to install Zoom on your device & have the Meeting ID & Password to log in. Have your earbuds or headphones ready.
  3. Get comfortable – Lie or sit down in a comfortable position, pets often enjoy snuggling up to their owners and sharing or amplifying the energy of distance Reiki sessions. Cats often seem to prefer distance Reiki sessions than dogs but it also depends on the pet.
  4. Open your mind to receiving healing energy – distance Reiki differs from in-person sessions in that (obviously) I’m not in the room with you. Otherwise the energy you receive from me is the same. Because most people aren’t familiar with the concept of a healing modality being able to transcend space and time to affect someone far away, a sort of leap of faith is involved. Saying an affirmation before your session can help your mind accept the energy that’s being transmitted to you, such as “I’m open to receiving healing on the physical, emotional, and mental levels” or simply “I’m open to whatever is ready to heal in my mind and body at this time.”

question: I've completed my session, now what?
answer:
Take a moment to write down any thoughts that popped up during your time, often the most golden ideas come to you in these quiet moments.

After your session, it’s always good to drink a glass of water when there has been energy movement. Go about your day, and take note of anything that comes up; you might think of a particular situation or person, something that’s been bothering you that you are ready to address, or you might have some ideas and inspiration drop in.

Reiki is a wonderful way to experience that feel good, pick me up, all is ‘right with the world’ harmony you feel when your energies are balanced, mind, body and spirit. By doing regularly scheduled sessions, you can enhance that wonderful feeling of Harmony.

question: How many sessions am I likely to need?
answer: This depends on what you are hoping for from Reiki. As a natural healing method, it is best to allow some time for Reiki to have an effect, so a series of treatments is going to be more beneficial than a single treatment in most cases.

question: Do you offer Reiki packages?
answer:
YES! Many people want to come for sessions weekly, bi-weekly and monthly. Healing packages are offered & what is best for you may be different for sometone else so let’s discuss it further so we can embark on a journey of deep healing together.

A healing package is a gift to yourself and a commitment to your healing process. the package sessions are designed to be used either weekly or bi-weekly to have the greatest healing impact.

question: How do I prepare for a community sound immersion?
answer:
• Be well hydrated and not hungry. Avoid eating a heavy meal right before, but we want to make sure hunger doesn’t distract you during the sound bath.
  • If possible, refrain from caffeine and alcohol consumption at least one hour prior to the sound bath.
  • Take time to reflect on any beliefs or thoughts you would like to surrender and intentions you would like to set.

question: What do I bring to a community sound immersion?
answer:
The following items are provided and you are welcomed to bring your own to make your most comfortable:

  • Water
  • Yoga mat
  • Blanket
  • Pillow

The following are other items you may want to bring and are not required.

  • Eye shade
  • Head pillow
  • Own yoga mat
  • Extra blanket
  • Personal journal/diary and pen

question: What if I'm attending virtually/online?
answer:
Great! You can join from anywhere.

Here are some tips to help you prepare:

  • Have the Zoom app downloaded and installed on your computer/phone/tablet/device. Please enable “Original Sound On” in your Zoom settings. If you’re not sure how to or if this feature is enabled in your Zoom settings, please see Zoom Original Sound On Instructions. Test out your Zoom audio and video settings prior to class.
  • Access the Zoom meeting ID and password or zoom link. This information is provided once you sign up for the event. If you cannot attend live and plan to watch and listen to the replay, access to the replay will be emailed to you approximately 24 hours after the event.
  • Set up your relaxation space – choose a place where you feel comfortable, relaxed, and safe. You’ll want a pillow for your head and perhaps a pillow or bolster for under your knees if your low back gets tense from lying down. If you choose to be seated, make sure you choose a comfortable armchair or recliner where you can fully relax. Grab a blanket even if your space is warm, since your body may go through temperature changes during the sound immersion and you might get chilly. An eye pillow if optional, but a great way to relax your eyes and forehead and having your eyes closed can help you focus more on your body’s sensations and feelings.
  • Turn off notifications and limit distractions – this is your relaxation / Zen time so turn off or mute your devices, and let your family or roommates know that you’re not available during your session time. If the dog needs a walk, do that before your session so they can relax too.
  • Have your earbuds or headphones ready. Headphones can improve the quality of sound.
  • Get comfortable – Lie or sit down in a comfortable position.
  • Open your mind to receiving healing sound. Saying an affirmation before your session can help your mind accept the energy that’s being transmitted to you, such as “I’m open to whatever is ready to heal in my mind and body at this time.”

question: What is Kundalini Yoga?
answer: Kundalini yoga is known as the yoga of awareness. Kundalini yoga gives you a strong foundation of health and well-being that gives you the energy to deal with all the physical, mental and spiritual facets of your life. Practices incorporate breath-work (pranayama), postures (asana), meditation (samadhi), mantra (sound) and deep relaxation (shavasana). The practice of kundalini yoga is best set on developing and nurturing our subtler sides, emphasizing the development of the nervous system & the mind and maintaining a healthy spine.

question: How do I prepare for a Kundalini Yoga class?
answer:
It is said all you need to have to attend a Kundalini Yoga class is the will to transform. All are welcome! No experience necessary!

A typical kundalini yoga class is as follows:

  1. Tune-In, intention setting exercise with ‘Ong Namo Guru Dev Namo’
  2. Warmup spinal movements to warm up
  3. Kriya, a sequence of exercises designed to produce specific effects (e.g., detoxing the liver, boosting willpower). Some classes may be largely seated and others much more rigorous on the body
  4. Relaxation/Savasana, often accompanied by the gong
  5. Meditation, mental and/or breathing exercise
  6. Closing affirmation, gratitude exercise. ‘Long Time Sun’, & mantra ‘Sat Nam’. ‘Long Time Sunshine” words: “May the Long Time Sun shine upon you. All love surround you and the pure light within you, guide your way on.”

question: What can happen as a result from a regular Kundalini practice?
answer:
• Increased vitality and stamina.
  • Strengthened nervous & glandular system giving you a strong immunity.
  • Reduced stress and release of old patterns and limitations.
  • Cultivation of inner strength and able to meet challenges in your life.
  • Calm the mind & connect with inner peace.
  • Opening of our heart to infinite source of love.
  • Awakened awareness and consciousness of your True Self.

question: Can I practice if I'm pregnant?
answer:
Becoming pregnant does not mean that you should stop your Kundalini Yoga practice. Usually it is ok to proceed as normal during your first trimester, but you should always consult a midwife or doctor just to make sure. Modify your exercises as your size increases. It is always recommended that you avoid navel exercises, inverted postures and strenuous kriyas. Pre natal women can enjoy its focus on creating inner strength, fearlessness, a strong physical body and a quiet mind. Squats are an excellent preparation for childbirth.

Please let your teacher know if you are pregnant. There are certain postures and breath techniques that are contraindicated in pregnancy.

It is generally recommended to go to a specialist class for pregnancy that tailors the class to suit your needs.

Ladies during their menstrual cycle are advised to avoid inverted postures, navel pumps, leg lifts and any strong breathing practice such as the breath of fire. However, this is a very individual decision.

question: Is Kundalini a religion?
answer: Kundalini Yoga is not a religion. Although some practitioners of Kundalini Yoga have adopted the Sikh faith, the practice is fundamentally non-religious, and equally suitable for those of all faiths and none. It provides a firm inner foundation for a spiritual life, awakening your soul and spirit, no matter which religion, if any, you may choose to follow.
